name: GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop
name_original: Ruta de las ermitas: Mare de Déu de la Salut y ermita de Bruguers — circular por el Arco de Bruguers
description: Hike the GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop, a moderate 3.9-mile trail with natural arches and panoramic views in Parc del Gar
tips: The hermitage of Bruguers is a Romanesque hermitage from the 13th century that stands out for the beautiful reddish color of the rock. It is located in a beautiful natural environment, in the municipality of Gavà.
Beautiful stone arc and view over the Llobregat
Beautiful cave that is located on one of the slopes of the Serra de les Ferreres, some rocks facing the sea and where, together with others, they form an area full of chasms and caves.
Nice hermitage on an easy path to the Fuente del Ferro. Can't see inside. The area is exquisite in native vegetation of the area.

faqs.question: Where is the trailhead and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop typically starts near the Ermita de Bruguers. You can usually find parking in the vicinity of the hermitage, which serves as a common starting point for this route.

faqs.question: Is the GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through the Garraf Natural Park, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the varied terrain and potential for other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot with little shade, and water sources are scarce, making it more challenging. Winters are generally pleasant but can be cooler.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or Parc del Garraf?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop or to access the Garraf Natural Park.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse terrain, starting with reddish sandstone and transitioning to white calcareous (limestone) areas with prominent karst formations. The paths can be rocky, with significant ascents and descents, and some sections may have uneven steps and rock slabs. It passes through dense Mediterranean scrubland.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Ermita de Bruguers, the striking natural rock formation of the Arc de Bruguers, and the Cave of the Virgin of Bruguers. You'll also pass near the Hermitage of Our Lady of Health and enjoy panoramic views of the Baix Llobregat region, the Mediterranean Sea, and even Barcelona.

faqs.question: Does this loop connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is part of the larger GR 92 long-distance path, which traces Spain's Mediterranean coast. It also intersects with other local trails such as Catalunya E20, Catalunya E21, Camí medieval a l'Eramprunya, Camí de Can Tries a Ca n'Amat, and Camí del Castell.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Facilities such as cafes or public restrooms are limited directly at the Ermita de Bruguers trailhead. It's advisable to plan accordingly and use facilities in nearby towns like Gavà before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty, rocky terrain, and lack of shade, you should bring sturdy hiking shoes, plenty of water (as there are no water sources on the trail), sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and snacks. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the GR 92: Ermita de Bruguers - Arc de Bruguers Loop is a circular route, meaning you will end up back at your starting point near the Ermita de Bruguers.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered moderate to demanding due to rocky sections, ascents, and descents. While not technically difficult, it requires good physical fitness and sure-footedness. It might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners, especially in warm weather due to the lack of shade and water.

waypoints.name: Cave of the Virgin of Bruguers
Hermitage of Our Lady of Health
